name: canonify-objects-actions
description: Deep-dive reference + tutorial for declaring ObjectTypes and ActionTypes in Canonify. Covers typed object shape (property kinds, enums, links, cardinality, on_delete), property visibility for the auth/read context, auto-CRUD overrides, and ActionType nuances — idempotency postures, governance, transition markers, and handler-DSL gotchas — with copy-paste recipes that decode against the live effect/Schema validators. Read after the base `canonify` skill.

resources.title: Object property types
resources.description: The property kinds (mapped / derived / computed), the seven PropertyTypes, enum narrowing rules, and nullable tightening — what catalog.refine_object_type accepts and why the validator rejects a narrowing.

resources.title: Object links & cardinality
resources.description: Declaring typed links between ObjectTypes: target, via (the FK column on the target), cardinality one|many, and the on_delete referential policy (restrict | cascade | set_null) honoured by every typed delete.

resources.title: Object property visibility
resources.description: The hidden flag and the confidential ObjectType marker — how a column is surfaced or withheld from the typed read shape, how confidential types default-deny without a grant, and how access shows up in the audit trail.

resources.title: Action input & governance
resources.description: The actions.declare envelope around a handler: the JSON input descriptor (types, enum, optional, format), governance (allowed_principals, requires_approval, requires_approval_when), idempotency postures, and the transition marker.

resources.title: Action handler patterns
resources.description: Copy-paste handler-DSL recipes: read-then-update, multi-table atomic insert with returns, find-or-create via read_optional, assert preconditions, read_aggregate, arithmetic + date expressions, when-guards, and on_fail — plus the gotchas the typecheck pass rejects.

resources.title: E-signature actions
resources.description: The built-in e-signature action surface you invoke (not declare): signature_request.create/send/view/sign/decline, signature.verify/certificate, the multi-signer signature_envelope.create/send/countersign/download/my_pending_countersigns/decline/void, and member_signature.set/get — their inputs, the request/envelope status enums + parts_frozen_when, consent + link_token persistArgs redaction + server-derived RequestContext evidence, and the terminal decline/void lifecycle.
